Application Field Selection Details

ENVF

FF

/NXI/TP1FF

Function Field Details

ENVF

FS

/NXI/TP1FS

Function Field Selection Details

ENVF

AL

/NXI/TP1AL

Application Logs

 

ALMSG

/NXI/TP1ALMSG

Application Log Messages

 

Note:
All entities are available for CRUD calls, except for ENVFHV which is READ only.

Updated Action:

  • CloneInstance:

Example call:

/sap/opu/odata4/nxi/p1_services/default/nxi/p1_processing/0002/CloneInstance

This call creates one or more process instances based on an existing process instance.

Request Body:

{
"envId":"BW4",   (Mandatory: needs to be in /NXI/TP1ENV)
"version":"0004",   (Mandatory: needs to be in /NXI/TP1ENV)
"procId":"0000111",  (Optional: if not passed, gets next available one, if passed, deletes existing and creates it new)
"shouldValidateOnly":"",  (Optional: If ‘X’, simulates a run, no changes to application persistence layer.)
"parameters":"I_PARAM,ARTC4",  (Optional: format: field1,formula1;field2,formula2)
"selections":"YF_PROD,I,EQ,PR03,;YF_PROD,I,EQ,PR02,", (Optional: format:
field,sign,option,low,high;field,sign2,option2,low2,high2)
“copiesNumber”:””, (Optional: If empty, defaults to 1. Determines how many instances are created. Between 1 and 50.)
"startDate":"20240326",  (Optional: Updates start date of the instance)
"description":"Test Description",   (Optional: Updates description of the instance)
"procType ":"20240326",   (Optional: Updates process type of the instance (RUN or SIMULATION))
"procState":"20240326",   (Optional: Updates process state of the instance between: ACTIVE,OPEN,DEPLOYED,SUSPENDED,COMPLETED,ABORTED)
"fromProcId":"0000110", (Mandatory: Must be an existing process instance)
}

Expected response: String with the newly cloned instances.

Manage and Deploy Processes

Enhanced screens are now available to handle updated processes and activities in SAP Profitability and Performance Management.

Sample Content

The sample content offer insights and recommended approaches for structuring the system in relation to subjects and sectors.

Below is a list of enhanced versions of the standalone sample content environments available with SP25.

Sample Content

ID

Version

OECD BEPS 2.0 Tax Calculation and Reporting

SXE

8

OECD BEPS 2.0 Tax Calculation and Reporting Powered by SAP Group Reporting Data Collection

SXE

GR04

ESRS Disclosure Management

SXQ

3

We really appreciate your time in reading this blog post! Please stay tuned for more upcoming improvements and enhancements in our future releases of SAP PaPM 3.0.

If you have any inquiries related to this topic within the SAP Community, you can find answers in the Q&A section using the provided tag link.

entry.title: SAP PaPM On-Premise: Exporting Environments
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

With the emergence of SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud sometime 3 years ago against the backdrop of a constantly evolving On-Premise solution, those who have already established a great deal of their calculations in On-Premise intended for PoC’s, demos or for any other valuable purpose might probably have the intention of preserving these important environments and reuse them at some point in Cloud, perhaps to consider switching over or for starters, just to explore the intricacies in Cloud.

Having the concept of an unnecessary reconfiguration of the entire model from ground up but at the same time guiding users when it comes to required changes in modeling along with other considerations, SAP Profitability and Performance Management has designed exactly the right applications enabling the convenient and efficient export and import of important environments from On-Premise to Cloud by way of the Export Environment (On-Premise) and Content Management (Cloud) applications, hence, making these important environments reusable from one platform to another.

This blogpost intends to present from an On-Premise standpoint, the export side of the export-import concept which will be made possible through the Export Environment application. So, if the application is still quite new to you or you belong to those who have already tried it but there are still some areas which you are not totally familiar with – I’ve got you covered.

Meanwhile, the Content Management application is a totally different topic of which relevant information is provided separately in another blogpost (see SAP PaPM Cloud: Introducing the New Content Management Screen).

Export Environment Application

Introduced as one of the new enhancements via On-Premise’s SP17 release, the application is found and can be launched via SAP Menu à Profitability and Performance Management à Administration à Export Environment or via transaction code /NXI/P1_FW_EXP_ENV.

 On the Export Environment screen, the only required information are the following:

  • Env.+Version (EEEVVVV) which stands for the environment and version
  • Directory for Download which can be any local folder where the compressed .json files will be placed

The application is capable of "exporting" or the compression of a chosen environment into a collection of .json files enclosed in a file Folder, saved in a preferred local directory.

 

 

 

 

 

Note: This file will then be used by the Content Management application to extract the environment during its import procedure and make it available in Cloud.

More than the application’s export functionality, it further comes with built-in consistency check which essentially acts like a “triage” – further driving the main objective of only exporting consistent environments down the line which in principle, is meeting the following requirements:

  • All functions are active.
  • Configurations and settings are supported in Cloud.

Consistency checks ensure that all functions are active. Hence, corrections in environment setup, settings and configurations are ensured first by the user to guarantee that each environment for export is properly configured and consistent.

Consistency Checks

A chosen environment to be exported is subjected to the following checks, ensuring the said environment’s reusability in PaPM Cloud. Only when an environment has undergone and satisfied these consistency checks shall it be successfully compressed to a zip file and for that matter, considered to be successfully exported.

Otherwise, the Export Environment application displays message logs:

  • A Warning in case the system detects indicators of a badly designed environment which would have an impact in terms of performance.
  • An Error pointing out inconsistencies, guiding the user further when it comes to the required modifications before the environment can be re-exported.
Warning messages

In PaPM modeling, there are generally quite a number of considerations to have a so-called optimized calculation model/modeling environment, including HANA DB parameters, correct sizing, volume of data involved in certain calculations, materialization of data involving just the right number of functions, and so on, to name a few.

Within the Export Environment application, the runtime performance may be impacted by sub-optimal modeling, and so the system gives information to the user that essentially implies for possible improvements in re-designing the model:

  • An increasing number of functions > (greater than) 200
  • Fields > (greater than) 1,000
  • A Join function containing > (greater than) 15 rules 

Error Messages

The consistency check as it was stated helps ensure that all functions are activated for a specific environment being considered for export. Since the concept of export from PaPM On-Premise would imply the environment being imported to PaPM Cloud at a later time, the consistency checks also block further unsupported data sources, fields, connections, and functions.

  • Inactive functions

  • Function type set to ‘Default’ is not permitted, and is blocked by the application.

Note: This is applicable to all functions (Allocation, Calculation, Transfer Structure, Join, View and Writer) with a corresponding function type setting in their Header section

For example, Allocation is the prescribed setting instead of the Default (Allocation).

Otherwise, the corresponding error log is displayed:

  • External data sources except for Model Table HANA is supported, but should be used with Connection Type “Logical”

  • Only environment fields and HANA fields are supported; hence, the application displays the corresponding error logs for other fields:

Sample error for BW fields

Sample error for BW InfoObjects

Sample error for DDIC fields

Sample error for Environment InfoObject fields

  • Unsupported functions

These are just some of the functions which are generally not supported in PaPM Cloud, and thus blocked by the application.

Sample error for Unsupported Functions

In a nutshell, the Export Environment application carries out the following consistency checks:

 

Prerequisites

Authorization

The necessary authorization for the Export Environment application is assigned to role /NXI/P1_ADMIN_USER_ALL.

SAP Notes

A list of notes are to be considered to take advantage of the application’s functionality along with its enhancements not available during the time of its actual release.

  • For SP16 and below, the following notes should be implemented along with its manual post implementation steps, if provided in the Manual Activities section of the note. 
    • SAP Note 3138297 - FS-PER Rel. 3.0 SP17: Export Environment
    • SAP Note 3209329 - FS-PER Rel. 3.0 SP18: Minor Improvements for Export Environment
    • SAP Note 3322703 - LOD-ANA-PER-OP 3.0 SP22: Export Environment Enhancements
    • SAP Note 3369372 - LOD-PER-OP 3.0 SP23: Export Environment, adjust Checks
  • For SP17 onwards, implement the notes as they are applicable.

There you have it. Feel free to share this blogpost with other colleagues in case you find it helpful. Should you have questions, feel free to raise them through https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html using the primary tag “SAP Profitability and Performance Management”.

You may also check out other interesting blogposts and other relevant information about SAP Profitability and Performance Management in the SAP Community page: https://community.sap.com/topics/profitability-and-performance-management 

Thanks a lot for your time and attention. Until next time, everyone!

entry.title: BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager powered by SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model
entry.content:
About me


Hello everyone, my name is Sandra Djurdjevic and for nearly two years, I’ve been deeply involved in analyzing the OECD BEPS 2.0 initiative and translating it to our SAP Profitability and Performance Management (SAP PaPM) solutions. Although I initially started this work as a highly technical person, I quickly found myself delving into the functional and tax aspects as well. With new challenges every day I can say that it never gets boring.


Recently Universal Model feature for SAP PaPM Cloud was released and to find more about it, you can visit this blog post: Unveiling the new functionality in 2024 of SAP PAP... - SAP Community.


We recognized the opportunity to seize it’s capabilities and incorporate OECD BEPS 2.0 initiative there as well. I'm thrilled to announce that the second version of this integration, known as BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager, has been officially launched. In this blog, I'll provide you with a deeper insight into its features and benefits.

 

Overview

 

The OECD's BEPS (Base Erosion and Profit Shifting) initiative, specifically Pillar 2, is a significant step towards combating tax avoidance and ensuring fairness in the global tax system. This initiative targets multinational corporations, particularly those operating in the digital economy, by introducing a Global Anti-Base Erosion (GloBE) minimum tax framework. Pillar 2 consists of two main components: the Income Inclusion Rule (IIR) and the Undertaxed Payment Rule (UTPR). The IIR requires jurisdictions to tax income of foreign affiliates if it's subject to low effective tax rates, while the UTPR allows jurisdictions to deny deductions or impose withholding tax on certain payments to related entities in low-tax jurisdictions. By implementing Pillar 2, countries aim to create a more level playing field, prevent profit shifting, and ensure that multinational corporations contribute their fair share to the societies in which they operate.


To find more about this topic you can refer to this blog series -> OECD BEPS2.0: More than a hot topic! - SAP Community.

SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model is offering many enhanced and useful functionalities such as transactional schema for data storing, granular role templates, real time calculations and improved data integrity measures. These updates mark a significant step towards facilitating transactional processing within the SAP PaPM Cloud, ensuring efficiency and reliability in day-to-day business operations.

 

SAP PaPM Universal Model BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager Content

 

As already said, with the introduction of the new SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model, we have spearheaded the development and implementation of pioneering solutions that harness its full suite of capabilities. This initiative represents a significant advancement in addressing diverse real-world challenges and delivering innovations tailored to meet the evolving needs of our clientele.

I am pleased to present the integration of the BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager into our Universal Model. This comprehensive solution encompasses all previously outlined functionalities, furnishing you with effective and dependable instruments for overseeing your tax responsibilities. With capabilities spanning from data validation and manipulation to intricate calculations and top-up tax allocation, you now have a consolidated array of tools and resources readily accessible.

Let me show you how it all works.

 

Reporting

 

The primary application utilized within the BEPS 2.0 content for comprehensive oversight is the “Manage Reports” tool. It enables users to access predefined dashboards or create customized ones tailored to their specific needs. The initial page offers a comprehensive overview, detailing confirmed and completed activities and those pending user action, facilitating seamless tracking of responsibilities throughout the BEPS process.

 

These actions can encompass a variety of tasks, contingent upon one's business role, ranging from data upload and review to manual adjustments and execution of calculations like the one for jurisdiction top-up tax. By simply clicking on any listed activity, users are seamlessly directed to the “Manage Activities” application, where they can promptly execute necessary actions, for example adding additional manual adjustments where required.

 

 

 

Real time calculation

 

Upon confirming the manual adjustments activity, you will observe that several subsequent tasks are executed automatically. This functionality, known as real time calculation, enhances efficiency by promptly processing tasks that can be automated based on the provided data. When additional input is required, the calculation process halts until the next responsible party confirms their activity and supplies the necessary information. Every time your action is required you will receive email notification providing status update.

 

 

Data validation

 

While we are on the topic of manual adjustments, I will use the opportunity to mention another noteworthy feature that underscores the importance of maintaining clean and accurate data. Should an attempt be made to input manual adjustments for items not present in BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager, the system will promptly issue a notification, alerting users to the discrepancy and advising them to reassess the data inputted. This robust data validation mechanism not only ensures data integrity but also streamlines the reviewer's task, fostering a more efficient workflow.

 

 

Data privileges

 

What is important to highlight is also the data privileges feature. While it may appear less prominent, it holds the potential to significantly enhance the workflow for tax experts. For instance, focus of tax expert overseeing solely Bulgarian entities may not extend to gathering data and making decisions for other jurisdictions. This is the reason why you have the possibility in BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager to define precise user authorization and filters. By leveraging this functionality, you gain tailored access to pertinent activities and entity data aligned with your specific responsibilities, optimizing efficiency and focus within the process.

 

 

Functional Scope

 

As we wrap up our exploration of the exciting new technical features in the BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager within the Universal Model, let's take a moment to highlight its functional scope. This powerful solution currently covers a comprehensive range of data points essential for calculating various KPIs as prescribed by the OECD. It supports diverse entity types such as Constituent Entities, Ultimate Parent Entities, Partially Owned Parent Entities, Joint Ventures, and Minority-Owned Entities.


Moreover, it addresses critical aspects like the IIR rule for Top-Up Tax allocation, Transitional Safe Harbor tests, and various Elections, including De-minimis exclusions, Fair market value election, Excluded entities election etc. But our work does not stop here—stay tuned to discover what is coming in future releases.

 

Conclusion


In conclusion, BEPS 2.0 Tax Manager marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to providing innovative solutions for our clients. With enhanced functionalities and streamlined processes, we are empowering businesses to navigate the complexities of tax compliance with greater ease and efficiency. From simplified manual adjustments to improved tracking of activities, this solution not only ensures compliance with BEPS standards but also promotes transparency and security in business operations. As we continue to evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape of global taxation, we

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model OData Integration with S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
There are several strategies on how to read and manipulate data from external application in SAP PaPM Cloud. However there are instances wherein if database are not exposed, we are only limited to do specific pulling mechanism. In this blogpost, I will share with you step by step guide on how you can leverage S/4HANA Cloud APIs to be used in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model (SM) and Universal Model (UM).

 

Prerequisite

  1. You have access with proper admin roles to an S/4HANA Public Cloud system
  2. You have destination administration access to SAP BTP where your SAP PaPM Cloud resides. Including SAP PaPM Cloud roles for Modeling and Connection configuration.  

 

Scenario:

User1 is tasked to read general ledger account line items from S/4HANA Public Cloud System. User1 saw in the help portal documentation that there is an API OData V2 which can be used for it which is G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X). User2 on the other hand wants to read consolidation task and assignments of consolidation document type and found an API Odata V4 which can be used: Consolidation Task ‒ Read

Both users would want to use SAP PaPM Cloud SM or UM to check if they can read on through using above API Service.

Read on through for the steps 😊

S/4HANA Cloud Setup

There are three main setup to be done here namely: Maintaining Communication User, Creation of Communication System and Creation of communication arrangements, read on below to see how it can be done:

1.      Create Communication User

  1. In your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, go to Communication Management .
  2. Choose Maintain Communication Users Tile
  3. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter your desired UserName, Description and Propose a password. Please take note to save the password into a secured location on your local since it will not appear again once you hit Create.
  4. Choose Create

2.      Create Communication System

  1. Go back to Communication Management and choose Communication Systems Tile
  2. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter your desired System ID. System Name will automatically be populated. Choose Create
  3. Since we are only aiming for inbound connection to PaPM Cloud, choose the checkbox for Inbound Only.
  4. Choose Users for Inbound Communication tab and Choose ‘+’ button.
  5. Add the Communication User created in Step 1 then choose OK.
  6. Choose Save.

 

3.      Create Communication Arrangement

  1. Go back to Communication Management and choose Communication Arrangements Tile
  2. Choose New and in the dialog box which appears enter the Scenario and desired Arrangement Name.
    Note: You can verify what Communication Scenario you need in the API Resource Attribute section in the api documentation. See example below.
  3. Choose Create
  4. In the required field communication System, Choose the Communication System you created from Step 2.
  5. Please take note of the API-URL as you would need this to create SAP BTP Destination in a later step.
  6. User Name will be populated automatically because it have assigned it in the selected Communication system.
  7. Please take note of the Service URL/Service Interface information as you would need this to populate information of SM or UM connections in a later step. In addition, be aware that this part https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com changes depending on your S/4HANA Cloud system.
    e.g.

    Service

    Application Protocol

    Service URL/Service Instance

    G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X)

    Odata V2

    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM    

    Consolidation Task ‒ Read

    Odata V4

    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

h. Scroll down to Outbound communication section. This is required only if you will do outbound scenarios however this blogpost is only limited to inbound scenario. In order to bypass the required User Name field, untick all Outbound Service Status.

i. Choose Save.

j. Perform steps a-i for the other required Communication Scenario if needed.

 

BTP Destination Configuration

Use below information to create Destination relevant to bridge S/4HANA Public Cloud to SAP PaPM Cloud.

  1. In SAP BTP Cockpit, go to the subaccount and Choose Destinations.
  2. Choose Create Destination and populate with below information

    Name

    Unique name of destination in current subaccount.
    e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Type

    Select “HTTP”.

    Description

    Any description for the destination name.

    URL

    API-URL from Step 3e of Create Communication Arrangement
    e.g. https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com

    Proxy Type

    Internet

    Authentication

    BasicAuthentication

    User

    User created from Step 1c of Create Communication User
    e.g. PAPMCS4_TECHUSER

    Password

    Proposed Password from Step 1c of Create Communication User

  3. Choose Save.

SAP PaPM Cloud SM Modeling – Read Data via Odata Connection

As you may have already know, SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model only supports Odata type V4 hence in this example, we will be using the information from Consolidation Task ‒ Read API.

  1. Login to Standard Model and go to Connection Management Application. By following the procedure on help portal documentation, Create an Odata Connection based from below data:

    Connection Name

    Unique name of connection in the tenant

    Connection Class

    Select “Default”

    Connection Source

    Select “Odata”

    Connection Description

    Any phrase you wish to describe details about the connection

    Destination Name

    Destination created from previous step e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Entity Set

    Service structure entity of the S/4HANA Cloud API e.g. ConsolidationTask

    Service Root

    Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

    Maximum page size

    1000 (Default)




  2. In a Created Environment, configure a Model View Odata and feed the information of the connection created above. Activate the Model View Odata and choose ‘Run’ to populate the data.


SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling – Read Data via Odata Service Connection

OData V4

SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model supports both Odata V2 and Odata V4 and in terms of configuration they follow the same steps. Let’s take a look if we can load the data of both APIs mentioned above.

  1. Login to Universal Model and go to Connection Application. By following the procedure on help portal documentation, Create an Odata Service Connection based from data below:

    Environment

    Environment ID to where a connection will be created
    e.g. ODATAV4CON

    Connection Target Type

    Select “Odata Service”

    Connection Name

    Unique name of Connection within the Environment
    e.g. S/4HANACloud to Universal Model OData V4

    Description

    Any phrase you wish to describe details about the connection

    Model Filter

    Used to filter out the models coming from the target to the ones which match the expression (pattern). Supports JavaScript regular expressions. It is optional and can be empty.

    Destination Name

    Destination created from previous step e.g. S4toPaPMC

    Service Path

    Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
    https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_consolidationtask/srvd_a2x/sap/consolidationtask/0001/

    Default Key Field

    Existing environment field of data type “String” or “UUID” created by the user and not registered from a different connection.

    Parent Folder

    All models synced from the connection to the environment will have this folder function as parent. It is optional and can be empty.


  2. On Edit Mode, you can rename and remove the Models which you do not want to be added in the Environment
  3. Choose a Model e.g. ConsolidationTaskME. On the left hand side,
    a. Choose the checkbox
    b. Choose Add Fields button to register the field into the environment
    c. If you do not want to carry over the relation, you can uncheck this option.
    d. Apply Changes then Save on next dialog box.
  4. Choose Sync Model button to add the Model Entity to the environment.
  5. Setup Reader Function on top of the Model Entity. Make sure to add the fields in the reader which you want data to be populated. Activate the Environment and Run the Reader to populate data on Model Entity.

OData V2

Now that you are done with setting it up for ODataV4, How about for OdataV2?

Like I said previously, steps in setting up Odata V2 for SAP PaPM Cloud UM should be the same as Odata V4. The only difference here would be the Service Path information from S4HANA Cloud Service URL/Service Interface which classifies whether what application protocol is used in this case, I used below information:

Service Path

Specifies the root of service URL from Step 3g of Communication Arrangement e.g.
https://my300470-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_GLACCOUNTLINEITEM

Follow steps 2-5 from Odata V4 Setup in order to bring the Model Entity created from Odata Service Connection and use a reader to buffer the data to it.

User1 and User 2 has completed their activity! Hoooraay!

This sums up one way of using S/4HANA Cloud Data into SAP PaPM Cloud. By mastering the steps outlined, you can be better equipped in your implementation project. With due diligence in checking, you can also try out other available APIs and create your own personal guide. Like I always said, this is one way and there can be another and a more optimized one. Hope you enjoy and until the next one! 😎

entry.title: Teams Management, Data Privileges and Data Locks in SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Welcome to my blogpost for Teams Management, Data Locks, and Data Privileges in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud - UM).

Before I begin, just wanted to ask if you are now familiar with roles assignment for you to have access in certain applications? If not, I suggest that you check and go through the Roles overview in SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model blogpost as it provides important user specific role assignment before going to the Universal Model application. From that blogpost, it was explained how specific role template can be assigned to users to limit their system access. On top of that, it was also discussed that with the help of teams, some application objects such as environment can be further restricted by a specific team as well as the data coming from the Model Entity application. This will be helpful as we go along with this blogpost.

As these topics were covered from that blogpost, let me focus in my blogpost on the following topics about teams and how to apply it in the SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications.

A. What is teams management?
a.1 Creation of teams in the application.
a.2 Creation of user attribute in BTP.

B. Providing privileges and assigning restrictions through the following:
b.1 Application object control through Reader Team and Writer Team.
b.2 Information or data control through Data Privileges.
b.3 Information of data control through Data Locks.
b.4 Additional information: Combination of Data Privileges and Data Locks.

A. What is Teams Management?

Let me talk about what teams management is.

Teams Management provides additional security and control with the help of applications and information. These teams are groups of users that work together in environments, data, processes, activities, and reports. User can only see the activities that are assigned to their team.

Examples:

  • Modeler Team A can only see the environment they are modeling or part of, and Modeler Team B can only see their environment.
  • Team Philippines can only see data coming from the Philippines, and Team Germany can only see data coming from Germany.

I will discuss further on how to create teams in the application and where you can maintain the created teams.

a.1 Creation of teams in the application
Here’s how you can create a team in the SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model application:

i.  Go to Universal Model application and select Manage Teams from the Process Application.

ii. As an example, I have created two teams in the Manage Teams application.

  • Team: DEMOREAD
  • Team: DEMOWRITE

iii. Upon creation, you will notice that Authorizations section is empty, this is expected since there is no user assigned yet to the team.

For more information about Teams creation, see Manage Teams.

a.2 Creation of user attribute in BTP

The creation of roles for a specific team in BTP is extremely important because you will be able to increase security by limiting the user’s access in the applications.

As there are no user assigned to the team, I will demonstrate on how you can create a user and how it will be mapped in the Manage Teams application.

i. You can access the BTP cockpit via this link - SAP BTP Cockpit

ii. Once you are already in the BTP cockpit, choose the tenant where you want to create the team and follow the steps below on how you can create Role and Role Collections.

  • Role creation:
    Take note of the team you created in Manage Teams application as this is to be used as value in BTP to have it mapped successfully.
    For more information about Role creation, see Build User Attribute Roles
  • Role Collections creation:
    Note: You cannot assign a role directly to a user instead you have to first include the role in the Role Collection and assign the created Role Collection to a user.
    For more information about Role Collection creation, see Build Role Collections

iii. After the successful creation of the Role and Role Collections via SAP BTP cockpit, the team and the user added is now displayed and mapped under Authorizations of Manage Teams application.

For more information about assigning role and role collections in BTP, see User Management

Note:
The user will only be added to the list upon first login and visiting an application they have access to if the administrator cannot see the assigned user to the team. This is a security feature of the Manage Teams application.

Now that we have completed the process on how to setup teams, let us see where we can use them, how to grant privileges, and assign restriction in the data records as well as access limitation with the help of teams through the SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications.

B. Providing privileges and assigning restrictions through the following:

b.1 Application object control through Reader Team and Writer Team

Are you wondering how you can limit the users access in the application? How can read and write team affect a certain object and data? If you are interested in knowing the answers to these, you should continue with this blogpost.

To answer the questions, you can limit the users access in the application by assigning restrictions through Reader Team and Writer Team. You will find in the table an overview of expected behavior once users are assigned in teams in the application.  

 

Below is an example scenario wherein a power user such as MODEL_ALL can see and edit all 3 environments such as CAMPX, CAMPY and CAMPZ. Now let’s focus on environment specifics: 

  • CAMPX can only be seen or edited by TEAMA.
  • CAMPY can only be seen or edited by TEAMB.
  • CAMPZ environment can only be seen and edited by the environment owner (environment creator) since it is not assigned with any teams. 

USERS ASSIGNED TO MODEL_ALL ROLE (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)

TEAM A (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

TEAM B (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

CAMP Z (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS): Environment Owner (Environment Creator)  
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

We basically covered where teams can be used in the application. The usual question is: What exactly teams do and how it will affect the data records of the activity?

Let me discuss more about what teams can do, how to grant privileges, and assign restriction in the data records as well as access limitation through the following SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications:

b.2 Information or data control through Data Privileges 

To give you an overview of Data Privileges, it is an application that provides additional data protection and control of which data can a user see within the set of records. It allows the process owner to grant an authorization for certain users to have read and write access to the data records. If the users have read access, they will have the visibility to see the sets of records; then, if the users have the write access, they will be able to maintain and manipulate data records linked to a certain field and optionally to a function.

Note:
It is essential that a team is assigned for the data privileges to work.

Example:
If you are the process owner and you wanted to provide set of users who can only see specific data records, how can you do it in the application?

We will use this data records to showcase how data privileges work.

I will give you 2 different scenarios on how you can use the Data Privileges in the application with the help of teams created in the in the application.

Scenario 1: Reader Team Access

From the sample data records (above), the process owner will assign reader team to only have display access from the specific data records. We will use the Data Privileges application to be able to do that. See below configuration in the system:

  • Data privileges creation:
  1. Go to Manage Data Privileges application and choose Create.
  2. Provide the Runtime Environment to be used.
  3. Choose Continue.
  4. Choose an activity where configurations in data privilege shall be applied.
  5. Choose a function where the source of data record shall be applied for data privilege.
  6. Select the team you want to give access.
  7. Choose Create.
  • These are the objects used in data privileges creation:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below that the reader team will only have display access to:

As we have successfully created a data privileges entry for the reader team, I will show how data privileges affects data manipulation in the Manage Activities application for reader team.

It was expected that reader team can only have display access in the data records of the company PROJECTEK CORP but any modifications in the data records was not permitted and healthy error messages were issued by the application.

Scenario 2: Writer Team Access

At this point, you might be curious how the data privileges work when the process owner give the writer team the access to edit the activity data records. Assigning of writer team in data privileges has the same step as we did in the reader team.

We will use the fields and selections from the table below that the writer team will have edit access to:

After I have assigned a writer team and maintained the same fields and selections, let us try to see on what users can see and do in the data records of Manage Activities application.

You must take note that all users assigned in write access should have the read access too, to have the possibility to see the activity and data records.

Let us proceed to the Manage Activities application where the defined fields and selections in data privileges can be used.  

As you have seen, the writer team was able to manipulate the data records of company PROJECTSY GROUP as expected.

Since I already provided you two simple scenarios in this blogpost wherein, I explained the relevance of the reader team and writer team from data privileges perspective. I hope it provides enough information on how you can control and restrict your data records effectively. Here’s a short summary of the scenarios I discussed:

  • Users that are part of the reader team maintained in Data Privileges application will only have read access and cannot manipulate the data records.
  • If both users of writer team and reader team have been assigned in the Data Privileges application, only the users from writer team can manipulate the data records of the activity while users of reader team will only have read access.

Following this blogpost, I will teach you more on how you can provide a higher level of restriction on all exposed analytic and transactional data records using Manage Data Locks application in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model.

b.3 Information or data control through Data Locks 

Now you know that the data privilege grants access to see and edit specific data records by the reader team and writer team, our next topic will be assigning data locks to restrict updating the specific data records on top of the selection criteria given by the process owner to the writer team. 

Before we proceed, let me define what Data Locks is. It is an application in Universal Model that gives extra layer of protection to limit the editing access to the set of data records. It gives you the authority to lock the data to the assigned writer team users and restrict their access to edit and update the data records. You can set up data locking and specify areas of function and activity to lock, restrict, and unlock.

I will give an overview of what data locking can do to prevent changes to a specific data record.

Example:

If you are the process owner and you wanted to lock and restrict specific data records, how can you do it in the application?

We will use this data records to showcase how data locks work.

See below configuration oh how to enable data locks in the application:

  • Data Locks creation:
  1. Go to Manage Data Locks application and choose Create.
  2. Provide the Runtime Environment to be used.
  3. Choose Continue.
  4. Choose an activity where the configurations in data locks shall be applied.
  5. Choose a function where the source of the data record shall be applied for data locking.
  6. Select the team that is not allowed to perform configurations and data manipulation.
  7. Choose Create.
  • These are the objects used in data locks creation:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below to restrict access by the writer team:

Now let us proceed to the Manage Activities application where the defined fields and selections in data locks can be used.  

You must take note as well that all users assigned in write access should have the read access too, to have the possibility to see the activity and data records.

As a result, the writer team can see all the data records but limited to updating Year 2024 since the process owner locked the Year 2022-2023. Attempting to update the data records of Year 2022-2023 will trigger a healthy error message “FORBIDDEN”.

b.4 Additional Information: Combination of Data Privileges and Data Locks 

Now that we have covered on how giving privileges in the specific data records and assigning data restrictions via data locks application works, do you want to see how the application behaves if we combine giving privileges and data locking for writer team?

Example:

The process owner gave data privileges to two writer teams respectively Team A and Team B wherein both teams can only view and edit companies that they are assigned to.  With the given data privileges, the process owner will also be restricting data records modification for writer teams on certain years by assigning data locks.

We will use the fields and selections from the table below for Team A:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below for Team B:

Now let us see the results for both teams when the writer users try to access the data records with the given privileges and restrictions.

In summary, once the process owner maintains the user as part of the writer team in the Data Locks application and assigned fields and selection in a data, it will override the rights of the writer team users given in the Data Privileges application to perform data record manipulation even these users have the write access in the application.

Here are the following results of giving privileges and locking data in the same activity data records.

Now that you already know how you can limit the users access in the application and how reader and writer teams affect a certain object and data, you can start configuring your teams, data privileges and data locks in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model.

If you want to learn more about other applications in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model that can be used as information function and data enrichment function, follow, and track our upcoming blogposts for Collection and Model Collection functions.

I hope you learned and enjoyed reading this as much as I enjoy preparing this blogpost for all of you. Should you have any inputs, questions, and suggestions you can add a comment below 😊
